scenario_title: add_rr then delete_rr of the same RR in one UPDATE

group_1.behavior: Processes the UPDATE sequentially; the add is seen by the later delete, so the message succeeds and leaves no host2.campus.edu. A RRset.
group_1.post_state: host2.campus.edu. A is absent after the UPDATE.

group_2.behavior: Normalizes or validates the intra-message delete against pre-update state and rejects the self-canceling pair as an error.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3 and §3.4 define add_rr and delete_rr semantics, but they do not specify whether operations inside one UPDATE must be applied strictly in order, batched, or normalized before execution. As a result, the delete_rr may or may not see the RR added earlier in the same Update Section, producing divergent reply codes and/or observable final state.
expected_divergence.test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified intra-message ordering problem by using one UPDATE that first adds an A RR for host2.campus.edu. and then deletes the exact same RR. If the server applies operations sequentially, the net result is success with no lasting RRset; if it evaluates the delete against the pre-update state or normalizes the message, it can reject the UPDATE with NXRRSET or otherwise handle it differently. A follow-up QUERY on the touched name makes the post-state observable and keeps the oracle binary for Ferret.
